text:
The most common bug to avoid when implementing a matrix transpose function is incorrectly assuming that the input matrix is a square matrix or not handling the case where the input matrix is empty or has rows of different lengths. 

```python
def matrix_transpose(m):
    if not m or not all(len(row) == len(m[0]) for row in m):
        raise ValueError("Input matrix must be a list of lists with the same length")
    return [[row[i] for row in m] for i in range(len(m[0]))]
```
